lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the old man who used to be the doorman?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: You mean my father? He went into retirement, I've taken over the family business instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father! But you don't look anything like him!

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: No, fortunately for me I inherited my good looks from my mother.

sections.title: Attempting to open the Magic door

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Sorry, we cannot allow you to bring those sacks into the market for security reason.

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: You may not pass through this door without paying the trading tax.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how much is the tax?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: The cost is one diamond.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't brought my diamonds with me.

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: No tax, no entry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A diamond? Are you crazy?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Not at all. Those are the rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't brought my diamonds with me.

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: No tax, no entry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do with all the diamonds you get?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Ever heard of fairylights? Well how do you think we make 'em? First we collect a pile of gems and then we get a spider to spin 'em into a long web, we light the jewels by imbuing each one with a little bit of magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're telling me fairylights are made out of gems?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: That's right, how else could we make 'em twinkle so beautifully?

sections.title: Using Nuff's certificate on a gatekeeper next to Zanaris market

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what these strange marks mean?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: That you've been doodelling? If you don't mind I have a job to do, please stop distracting me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what these strange marks mean?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: No I don't and I'm trying to work here. Please stop distracting me!

sections.title: Gatekeeper near the entrance to the Tangleroot's lair

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing all the way over here? Shouldn't you be at the market door?

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Yes, that's my usual spot, but the Godfather said I should be here, so here I am.

lines.speaker: Gatekeeper
lines.text: Hello, did you just come from the market gates? Are my brothers doing their job well? I do worry that they'll start letting people into the market for free without me being there to supervise them!

sections.title: Attempting to enter the shady grove without Magic Secateurs

lines.speaker: Gate Keeper
lines.text: I'm afraid nobody is allowed in there, orders from the boss.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the grove after obtaining Magic secateurs

lines.speaker: Gate Keeper
lines.text: I've received orders to let you through if you want. Good luck.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the grove after defeating Tanglefoot

lines.speaker: Gate Keeper
lines.text: Be careful in there, there are still some of those baby Tanglefootii around.
